people.name: Cheryl Ann Bruce-Sanders
people.involvement: The central figure in the case. A victim of institutional failures, including financial abuse and neglect of her rights and health.

people.name: Andrew Bruce-Sanders
people.involvement: Cheryl's primary carer and advocate. Has been fighting to protect her rights, assets, and well-being against multiple institutional failures.

people.name: Dr. Harvey
people.involvement: Conducted a cognitive assessment of Cheryl but his report was used out of context at QCAT. A re-referral has been pending for 10 months.

people.name: Zachary Hutchins
people.involvement: Initiated the QCAT proceedings that stripped Cheryl of her rights without her knowledge. Misrepresented her capacity and coordinated with Bank of Queensland.

people.name: Bec O'Connell
people.involvement: Pressured for Cheryl's removal from her SMSF, creating a crisis that was used against Andrew at QCAT.

people.name: Leitzel Kidd
people.involvement: An advocate from ADA Australia who is horrified by the case and is providing support.

institutions.name: Home Instead Brisbane West
institutions.failures: Took $9,000 and provided no service for over two years, after placing Cheryl in a mold-infested property.

institutions.name: Royal Brisbane and Women's Hospital (RBWH)
institutions.failures: Employed Zachary Hutchins, who acted unethically and without due diligence, leading to the QCAT scandal.

institutions.name: Bank of Queensland
institutions.failures: Created a financial crisis around the SMSF and coordinated with the RBWH social worker to pressure the family.

institutions.name: Queensland Public Trustee
institutions.failures: Mismanaged Cheryl's finances, depleted her funds, and overstepped their legal authority by acting as a guardian.

institutions.name: Comminsure/Hollard
institutions.failures: Delayed property repairs for four years, failed to identify black mold, and improperly closed claims.

institutions.name: Queensland Civil and Administrative Tribunal (QCAT)
institutions.failures: Made a decision to appoint the Public Trustee without ensuring Cheryl was notified of the hearing, a fundamental breach of justice.
